framework: Claude for Financial Services — equity-research methodology (/screen, /sector, /comps, /catalysts, /thesis). Data: Robinhood fundamentals + web_search (no paid-vendor MCP connectors).
action: Catalyst-week rotation on a soft-CPI regime turn: exit GILD (no dated event) and trim LLY (its July catalysts resolved and the tape is fading), recycle into META ahead of its 7/29 print and a small PYPL position into 7/28 where management already lowered the bar; hold BAC after today's beat, hold both semi event bets (ASML prints 7/15, TSM 7/16) and the CVX oil hedge.
sizing: 64% deployed across 8 names (BAC 12, LLY 10, ASML 8, TSM 8, REGN 8, META 8, CVX 5, PYPL 5), 36% cash through two binary semi prints in the next 48h

regime.label: Soft June CPI kills the July hike scare — risk-on tape into a heavy catalyst week (banks beat today; ASML 7/15, TSM 7/16, then late-July mega-cap prints)
regime.note: June CPI fell 0.4% m/m vs -0.1% expected (3.5% y/y vs 3.8% exp; core flat m/m, 2.6% y/y vs 2.8% exp) — before the print, futures priced 74.9% hold at 3.50–3.75% with 25.1% odds of a HIKE, so the downside surprise removes the near-term hike tail and SPY is regaining ground. Big banks opened Q2 season with beats: JPM printed a record quarter ($6.14 vs $5.85 est on a ~$6B trading windfall) and BAC beat on both lines. Oil stays bid on the collapsed U.S.–Iran ceasefire (WTI ~$74). The unresolved risk is semis: the group is in a multi-week rout (MRVL -22.5%, ARM -12.6%, QCOM -9.4% over 20 sessions) and ASML/TSM report within 48h — those two prints decide whether the AI-capex trade re-rates or breaks further.

thesis: What changed since 7/13: the two macro overhangs the last report queued into — CPI and bank earnings — both resolved bullishly this morning (CPI -0.4% m/m, JPM record, BAC beat with the stock printing a fresh 52-wk high today), so the hawkish-Fed cash hoard is now pure drag and the book redeploys from 42% toward 36% cash. The core of the book is unchanged and event-driven: ASML (8%) reports tomorrow 7/15 pre-market with options pricing an 8.4% move — the bet is that bookings commentary confirms BofA's view that the 2027 backlog is effectively fully booked, against a consensus positioned for the semi rout to continue; TSM (8%) reports 7/16 with $39–40.2B guided revenue (+10% q/q) and 'extremely robust' AI demand language already on record, entered 12% below its 6/30 high so the rout has de-risked the entry. New money goes to two late-July prints the market is not yet positioned for: META (8%, reports 7/29) — the strongest clean momentum in the universe (+15.5% over 20 sessions, RSI 69, not extended) at 23.9x earnings; and PYPL (5%, reports 7/28) — 8.9x earnings with a new CEO, a $1.5B cost program, and management having already guided Q2 EPS down, meaning the print resolves against a lowered bar while the tape (+15.5%/20d) says positioning is already turning. Funded by exiting GILD (no dated event, defensive utility lower post-CPI) and trimming LLY 12→10 (Foundayo approval and the $50/mo Medicare GLP-1 bridge both resolved in early July; the stock has faded 5.4% from its 7/7 high — the ramp story now belongs to the ~Aug 6 print, so it keeps a large-but-smaller seat). BAC holds its full 12% through the beat (14.8x, NII + sales/trading both inflecting); CVX (5%) stays as the geopolitical hedge with WTI ~$74 and two fresh street upgrades ($210/$220 PTs).

evidence.point: June CPI: 'the Consumer Price Index declining 0.4% in June versus economist forecasts for a decline of 0.1%. On a year-over-year basis, CPI was up 3.5% versus forecasts for 3.8% and 4.2% in May'; core 'was flat in June versus forecasts of 0.2%'
Pre-CPI Fed pricing: 'a 74.9% probability the Fed holds rates steady at its current range of 3.50% to 3.75%, with odds of a quarter-point hike at 25.1%'

BAC Q2 (reported this morning): 'earnings of $1.21 per diluted share… beat estimates of $1.13'; 'revenue of $31,558,000,000, beating estimates of $30,728,693,389'; net income '+27% to $9.1B'; intraday the stock set a new 52-wk high of 61.21 today (7/14)

JPM Q2: 'EPS of $6.14 per share (excluding significant items) versus $5.85 expected, and revenue of $58.02 billion versus $50.19 billion expected… biggest quarterly profit on record, driven by a nearly $6 billion windfall from stock trading'

ASML 7/15 print: 'revenue guidance is 8.4 billion to 9 billion euros, with a gross margin of 51% to 52%'; 'Options traders are pricing in an 8.36% move'; 'Bank of America expected ASML's 2027 order backlog to be effectively fully booked before management reports Q2 results'

TSM 7/16 print: 'guided second-quarter revenues of $39-$40.2 billion, representing approximately 10% sequential growth at the midpoint, and second-quarter gross margin is projected at 65.5%-67.5%'; AI chip demand described as 'extremely robust'
META reports 'Wednesday, 29 July 2026, after the close'; screen shows +15.5% over 20 sessions vs SPY +1.5%, RSI 69, P/E 23.9 (Robinhood)

PYPL reports 7/28; 'management reiterated full-year guidance but warned of a tougher Q2 comp and a near-term EPS decline'; 'a material $1.5B cost-savings ambition' under new CEO Enrique Lores; P/E 8.94 (Robinhood), +15.5% over 20 sessions

CVX/oil: 'spot WTI climbing back into the mid-$70s after jumping about 5% to around $74 a barrel following the collapse of a ceasefire'; 'Wolfe Research upgraded Chevron to Outperform with a $210 price target'; RBC 'reiterated an Outperform rating and $220 price target'

LLY: 'FDA approved Foundayo (orforglipron)'; 'Medicare GLP-1 Bridge Program, set to launch July 1, 2026… $50 per month… approximately 20 million Medicare patients may meet the clinical criteria' — both now resolved; stock 1181.87 (7/13 close) vs 1249.45 52-wk high set 7/7 (Robinhood)

risks: ASML prints tomorrow with an 8.4% implied move — a bookings miss hits both semi positions (16% of the book) the same week; sized so a -8% ASML day costs the book ~0.6%.
TSM 7/16: strong guidance is already on record — an in-line quarter with no full-year raise could sell off in a routed sector.
BAC/banks: the beat is now the consensus — with the stock at a 52-wk high, mean-reversion risk replaces event risk.
PYPL is a value-trap risk: branded-checkout share loss is structural; the 8.9x multiple can stay cheap if the 7/28 print shows volume growth without margin stabilization.
CVX: a re-brokered ceasefire unwinds the geopolitical premium quickly (~5% of WTI's move was one headline).
Post-CPI risk-on can reverse on a single hawkish Fed speaker — the market still prices a September HIKE, not a cut.

data_examined.label: Full 100-name universe screened in randomized order: 5/20/60-session returns, 20d relative strength vs SPY, RSI(14), distance to 20d high, volume ratio
Robinhood fundamentals (P/E, P/B, 52-wk range, dividend, today's session) for BAC, ASML, TSM, LLY, GILD, REGN, CVX, META, PYPL, NET
June CPI print, Fed futures pricing, bank Q2 results (JPM/BAC), ASML & TSM earnings previews, META/PYPL earnings dates, oil/ceasefire coverage, MRNA breakdown (screened and rejected)
data_examined.source: web_search 2026-07-14 (CoinDesk, CNBC, Motley Fool, TechTimes, TipRanks, Yahoo Finance, Benzinga, investor.pypl.com, financecalendar.com)

rationale.BAC: Hold the full 12% through the beat. [DATA] Q2 EPS $1.21 vs $1.13 est, revenue $31.56B vs $30.73B est, net income +27% to $9.1B; the stock printed a fresh 52-wk high (61.21) this morning; still 14.8x earnings, the cheapest large-cap financial in the book. [ASSUMED] Post-beat drift in a soft-CPI tape outruns the mean-reversion risk at the high; no trim until the tape breaks.
rationale.LLY: Trim 12% → 10%. [DATA] Both July catalysts resolved: Foundayo (orforglipron) FDA-approved and the $50/mo Medicare GLP-1 bridge live since 7/1 (~20M eligible patients); the stock has faded from its 7/7 52-wk high of 1249.45 to 1181.87 and traded as low as 1140 today. [ASSUMED] The Medicare volume ramp is real but now reports through the ~Aug 6 Q2 print (≈16 ticks out) — a post-catalyst position this large is dead weight in the interim, so it stays core but smaller.
rationale.ASML: Hold 8% into tomorrow's print (7/15 pre-market). [DATA] Q2 guide €8.4–9B / 51–52% GM; options price an 8.36% move; BofA expects the 2027 EUV backlog effectively fully booked; entered at the 7/13 open under a 1815 limit, 13.7% below the 6/30 high. [ASSUMED] Consensus is positioned for the semi rout to continue — a bookings confirmation re-rates the whole AI-capex complex; this is the thesis's binary and it is sized for an 8% adverse move.
rationale.TSM: Hold 8% into Thursday's print (7/16). [DATA] Guided $39–40.2B revenue (+10% q/q mid) at 65.5–67.5% GM, 'extremely robust' AI demand on record; stock 421.58, 12% below its 6/30 high of 479 after the rout; 35x earnings. [ASSUMED] The pullback into a guided-up quarter is the market handing back the entry the June run never offered — position was entered at 460, and the print (not the rout) decides it.
rationale.REGN: Hold 8%. [DATA] 16.2x earnings, 2.2x book — the cheapest name in the book's health sleeve; +0.7% vs entry while the market chopped. [ASSUMED] The valuation floor keeps working without a near event; Q2 print early August is the next mark.
rationale.META: New 8% — the tick's top conviction. [DATA] Reports Wednesday 7/29 after the close (≈11 ticks); +15.5% over 20 sessions vs SPY +1.5% with RSI 69 (strong but not extended, unlike NET at 83); 23.9x earnings, roughly a market multiple for the best momentum in the universe. [ASSUMED] Consensus attention this cycle is on capex discipline; the ad-revenue acceleration the tape is sniffing resolves at the print before the crowd repositions — enter now, 15 days ahead, with a 663.30 limit so the position only opens near the price the thesis priced.
rationale.CVX: Hold 5% oil hedge. [DATA] WTI ~$74 (+5% on the ceasefire collapse, Brent +20% off its monthly low); Wolfe upgraded to Outperform ($210 PT), RBC at $220; 3.8% dividend yield while it works. [ASSUMED] The book's only positive-carry hedge against the Iran headline tail; a re-brokered ceasefire is the exit trigger, not a price stop.
rationale.PYPL: New 5% starter. [DATA] 8.9x earnings (vs BAC 14.8x, META 23.9x); management has already guided the Q2 print (7/28, ≈10 ticks) to 'a near-term EPS decline'; new CEO Enrique Lores since 3/1 with a stated $1.5B cost program; +15.5% over 20 sessions says positioning is turning first. [ASSUMED] A print that merely matches the lowered bar re-rates a single-digit multiple — the asymmetry is in the setup, not the business; the 7/28 result is the thesis test and a failed margin story is the exit.
rationale.GILD: Exit (5% → 0). [DATA] -0.9% vs entry with no dated catalyst on the calendar; 17.9x earnings, 2.45% yield. [ASSUMED] It was hawkish-regime ballast; after this morning's CPI the regime argument is gone and the book's scarce capital belongs in named events — sell the position that has no date, not the ones that do.

reflection.looking_back: The 7/13 report queued a full V exit, an ASML 8% entry (1815 limit) and a CVX 5% starter (178.2 limit); those settle at the 7/13 open this run. The carried book marked $87.16 into 7/13 — -12.8% all-time vs SPY +0.6% (alpha -13.4pts), with last tick's book -0.1% vs SPY +1.4% (alpha -1.5pts) and a -5.03 annualized Sharpe over 16 sessions [DATA: analyst_memory 7/14].
worked: Positioning BAC (12%) ahead of a dated print finally paid: this morning it beat on both lines ($1.21 vs $1.13 EPS, $31.56B revenue) and set a 52-wk high of 61.21 intraday [DATA: stockstory 7/14, RH fundamentals] — the anticipation discipline the 7/06 reflection demanded worked on its first full test.
The V exit logic held: V's +12.1% 20d strength never rebuilt the stablecoin-derating thesis, and the freed ~7% now funds this week's dated events [DATA: screen 7/14].
REGN, the valuation-floor sleeve, is +0.7% vs entry and green while three other carries marked red [DATA: analyst_memory] — cheap-with-a-floor remains the book's most reliable sleeve.
missed: TSM is -8.4% vs the 460.19 entry [DATA: analyst_memory] — the book rode the entire semi rout without resizing, and the position now needs Thursday's print just to get back to flat; the 7/06 report called the sector risk and did nothing about the size.

LLY is -3.2% vs entry and faded 5.4% from the 7/7 high [DATA: analyst_memory, RH] — held at full 12% weight AFTER its catalysts (Foundayo approval, Medicare bridge) resolved; a resolved catalyst is a sell-the-news setup and the book treated it as a hold-forever.

Cash sat at 42% through a tick where SPY rose 1.4% [DATA: analyst_memory] — the alpha hole widened for the same structural reason two reflections in a row have named: an under-deployed book cannot close a -13pt gap in an up-tape.
reflection.adjustment: Every position must now carry a date or an active driver: GILD (no event) exits, LLY (event resolved) shrinks, and the freed capital goes to four named prints inside 11 ticks (ASML 7/15, TSM 7/16, PYPL 7/28, META 7/29) — deployment rises 58%→64% and the remaining 36% cash is the event buffer, not a regime hedge.

charts.title: TSM daily close into the 7/16 print (the semi-rout entry)
P/E across the book — value funds the event bets
